senses.definition: A large wheel at the front of a horse-drawn cart, or a wooden wheel used on carts and wagons.
examples: The farmer attached a wooden cartwheel to the wagon.
Children pretended to ride in a cart as the cartwheel creaked.

senses.definition: A graceful acrobatic move where a person spins the body around by one hand while the body is horizontal.
examples: The gymnast showed a flawless cartwheel.
She landed the cartwheel after a swift run-up.

senses.definition: To perform a cartwheel; to spin the body in a parallel arc with hands on the ground.
examples: The kids cartwheeled across the lawn.
She cartwheeled off the bench in a playful moment.

senses.definition: Past tense of cartwheel; performed a cartwheel.
examples: She cartwheeled across the field.
They cartwheeled onto the stage in a playful entrance.

senses.definition: A person who performs cartwheels; a gymnast or playful performer who cartwheels.
examples: The little girl is a skilled cartwheeler.
In the parade, a group of cartwheelers entertained the crowd.

senses.definition: Plural of cartwheeler; multiple people who perform cartwheels.
examples: A line of cartwheelers flashed across the lawn.
The troupe included several cartwheelers.

senses.definition: Present participle of cartwheel; performing cartwheels, or moving like one.
examples: Children were cartwheeling in the park.
The dancers were cartwheeling across the stage.

senses.definition: More than one cartwheel; multiple wheels on carts or wagons.
examples: The cart wheels creaked as the wagon moved.
Old carts had iron-bound cartwheels.

senses.definition: A set of acrobatic moves where the performer rotates around their hands.
examples: The gymnasts showed stacked cartwheels and leaps.
She landed two cartwheels in a row.

senses.definition: A whip used to crack a cart or horse-drawn vehicle; a whip tied to a cart’s reins.
examples: The farmer checked the cartwhip before driving the horse.
A crack of the cartwhip signaled the team to move on.

senses.definition: A person who makes carts or wagons; a wagon-maker.
examples: The cartwright repaired several carts in the yard.
In old villages, the cartwright's shop was busy with frames and wheels.

senses.definition: The craft or process of making carts or wagons; work of a cartwright.
examples: Cartwrighting was a skilled trade in the town.
He studied cartwrighting to learn old wagon construction.

senses.definition: Plural of cartwright; multiple wagon-makers.
examples: The old village had several cartwrights in the guild.
Cartwrights supplied carts for farmers and traders.

senses.definition: A term historically used to refer to a cart with a hump or load; also a dialectal term for a heavy burden or cart. Note: usage is rare and largely archaic.
examples: An old text mentions a carty road used by traders.
The term carty appears in some dialect records.

senses.definition: Unclear or very rare term; not established in contemporary English usage.
Variant spelling of carucage; a medieval tax or tribute, usually payable by tenants on a carucate of land.
examples: The feudal record listed the caruage due from the manor.
Historically, carucage was one form of royal taxation.

senses.definition: A historical tax assessed on a carucate of land; a medieval land tax.
examples: Tax records describe the carucage levied on tenant farms.
Scholars study carucage as part of feudal fiscal systems.

senses.definition: Plural of carucage; historical taxes on land.
examples: Records show several carucages collected over the decades.
Researchers compare carucages across regions.

senses.definition: A Latin-derived term appearing in historical legal or fiscal contexts; extremely rare in modern usage.
A medieval title or term found in some Latin legal/fiscal texts; rare in modern English.
A measure of land in medieval England; amount sufficient to support a plough team.
examples: The manor included several carucates of land.
Carucate was used in feudal taxation and land assessment.

senses.definition: To assign or measure land in carucates; to tax or agrarian assessment by carucate.
examples: The survey carucated the estate for taxation.
They carucated the land according to feudal rules.

senses.definition: Past tense or past participle of carucate; measured or taxed by carucate.
examples: The manor was carucated for the annual levy.
They carucated the fields as part of the survey.

senses.definition: Plural of carucate; measures of land in medieval taxation.
examples: Several carucates were recorded in the land survey.
The estate consisted of multiple carucates.

senses.definition: Latin term found in some botanical or medical Latin phrases; not common in modern English usage.
A small, fleshy, rounded projection or gland at the edge of a body part in anatomy; also a small tumor-like growth.
examples: The doctor examined the caruncle near the eye.
In botany, a caruncle can be a small fleshy outgrowth.

senses.definition: Plural of caruncle; small anatomical outgrowths or glands in multiple places.
examples: The study noted caruncles on several plant seeds.
Some species have caruncles on their leaves.

senses.definition: Latin singular form for caruncle; used in some scientific terms.
Latin plural form for caruncula; used in scientific description.
